text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on breadth broadened, semis led, energy lagged.
U.S. equities got a clean bid on Iran-de-escalation hopes, with 63.5% of names up and IWM +0.93% beating SPY +0.39%. The tape was not a narrow mega-cap squeeze: ex-Mag7 median was +0.42% versus Mag7 median -0.12%, so participation improved while leadership rotated into small caps and cyclicals. Flows looked constructive, with higher volume in DIA, RSP, IJH, XLK, and XLE, while defensives stayed secondary.

INDICES — VIX was 16.7, down 1.0 from 17.6.
Best to worst: IWM +0.93%, DIA +0.60%, QQQ +0.42%, SPY +0.39%; Small > large by 0.51pp. TLT was the notable cross-asset move at +0.55%, while GLD -0.76% and USO -1.14% confirmed less fear and less inflation stress. SPY and QQQ were not flagged below their 200-day MAs.

BREADTH — 56.7% above SMA20, 61.5% above SMA50, 65.4% above SMA200.
Adv/dec was 132/76, pct_up was 63.5%, and uvol/dvol was 1.05, so internals were firm. Mag7 median 1d was -0.12% versus ex-Mag7 median +0.42%, a 0.54pp gap, and narrow_leadership=false; SPY matched the universe median by -0.03pp, so no index strong/internals weak read. Mega_cap_tilt=false, but a few mega-caps were not carrying the tape.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio was 0.8x and QQQ vol_ratio was 1.1x.
DIA 1.1x, RSP 1.1x, IJH 1.0x, XLE 1.1x, and XLK 0.9x were gaining attention; IJR 0.7x, XLP 0.7x, XLB 0.7x, and XLY 0.7x were being ignored. QQQ’s volume down with price up is a low-conviction rally, while SPY’s mild volume fade with price up also points to a measured bid, not a chase.

SECTORS — XLV Health Care +1.17%, XLK Technology +1.00%, XLU Utilities +0.78%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLE Energy +0.61%, XLB Materials +0.54%, XLF Financials +0.41%, XLY Discretionary +0.40%, XLP Staples +0.17%, XLRE Real Estate +0.13%, XLC Comms -0.55%.
Top Industries ETFs were CIBR +2.59% and ROBO +2.42%; bottom were KWEB -2.61% and UNG -3.44%.

ROTATION — Top groups were The Semi 7 +3.04% and The Software 7 +2.41%; weakest were The Old Metals 7 -0.21% and The Housing 7 -0.35%.
Industry flows showed SOXX +2.41% and DRIV +2.34% leading, while KWEB -2.61% and UNG -3.44% lagged; defensives_minus_cyclicals was +0.05pp, so no defensive risk-off signal. This was broad participation with AI hardware still the cleanest leader, not a narrow mega-cap tape.

THE 7s — Top 5: Semi 7 +3.04%, Software 7 +2.41%, Health 7 +1.99%, Buildout 7 +1.95%, Power 7 +1.50%.
Bottom 5: Medtech 7 +0.12%, Mag 7 -0.05%, Old Metals 7 -0.21%, Housing 7 -0.35%, Insurance 7 -0.42%.
Semi 7 was led by the strongest ticker, SOXX proxy strength via +3.04%, while Software 7 was led by INTU +4.19%; Mag 7 was weakest overall at -0.05%, with no clear internal split larger than the basket average.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is Jun 17, 2026, with CME at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%; hawk/dove/neutral count is 6/4/5.
USD liquidity stress was 37/100, labeled Normal, with the most-stressed component Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile. TED spread was 0.09% and ON-RRP was $1.0B, neither signaling acute stress.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol was 1.26 and QQQ PCR_vol was 1.57, both elevated and protection-heavy.
SPY skew was +4.0% and QQQ skew was +6.2%, so puts carried clear premium; VIX at 16.7 sat below its 20-day average of 17.6, so headline fear was contained. QQQ GEX flip was 625.0 versus spot near 29,481.64 for NDX, far from a gamma pin in index terms; MOVE at 78.4 was elevated versus 74.6, so bond vol stayed mildly stressed.

CROSS-ASSET — USO -1.14% and GLD -0.76% both fell while equities rose.
That combo says the Iran-risk premium bled out of oil and gold, while TLT +0.55% and UUP +0.14% kept the macro tone orderly. Lower energy fear cut inflation pressure, helping rate-sensitive and growth names.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 57.9/100, Neutral.
Top movers were INTU +4.19% in Software, SOXX +2.41% in Semis, and DRAM -2.80% in Semis. 20d momentum best was XLK +12.59% and worst was XLK? No, worst sector was XLC -0.07%; best basket was The Semi 7 +24.57% and worst basket was The Medtech 7 -5.24%. INTU’s 3.4x volume on +4.19% was accumulation, and IBM’s 2.7x on +0.34% was mild accumulation; Reuters-linked Iran headlines helped the risk-on bid.

WATCH — QQQ +0.42% needs follow-through because 1.57 PCR_vol and 0.8x volume say the rally was hedged and not crowded.
KWEB -2.61% deserves a closer look because 1.9x industry volume and a weak price signal point to distribution. UNG -3.44% is another watch because energy vol stayed high at OVX 76.0, but price broke down despite geopolitical noise.
